Author: John Edward
Title: How Solution Architects Can Use Microsoft’s Azure Globe Experience to Design Smarter Cloud Architectures

# How Solution Architects Can Use Microsoft’s Azure Globe Experience to Design Smarter Cloud Architectures

- by [John Edward](https://dellenny.com/author/delenyprogmail-com/)
- March 20, 2026March 20, 2026
- [Architecture](https://dellenny.com/category/architecture/), [Azure](https://dellenny.com/category/azure/)

Tags:[Azure](https://dellenny.com/tag/azure-2/ "Azure")[Solution Architecture](https://dellenny.com/tag/solution-architecture/ "Solution Architecture")

![](https://dellenny.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/azuredatacenters-300x182.jpg)

[previousSharePoint Hub Sites: Building a True Digital Workplace](https://dellenny.com/sharepoint-hub-sites-building-a-true-digital-workplace/)

In today’s cloud-driven landscape, solution architects are expected to make decisions that balance performance, scalability, compliance, cost, and even sustainability. It’s no longer enough to simply choose the right services—you also need to choose the right **locations**.

One of the most powerful yet often overlooked tools available is Microsoft’s Azure Global Infrastructure “Globe” experience. At first glance, it looks like an interactive map. But for a solution architect, it’s much more than that it’s a strategic planning tool that can shape better, smarter architectures from day one.

## What Is the Azure Globe Experience?

The Azure Globe experience is an interactive visualization of Microsoft’s global cloud infrastructure. It allows you to explore Azure regions, understand how they’re distributed geographically, and analyze the capabilities of each location.

Through this tool, you can:

- View Azure regions across the world
- Understand availability zones within each region
- Filter based on compliance, services, and capabilities
- Explore how regions are connected

Microsoft operates one of the largest cloud networks in the world, with dozens of regions and hundreds of datacenters. This global presence is what enables organizations to build highly distributed, resilient systems and the globe experience helps you tap into that potential.

## Why It Matters for Solution Architects

Designing cloud architecture is not just about picking technologies. It’s about making strategic decisions that impact users, businesses, and operations at scale. The Azure Globe experience helps architects make those decisions with clarity.

### Smarter Region Selection

One of the first and most critical decisions in any cloud project is choosing the right region.

With the globe tool, you can visually identify regions closest to your users, which directly impacts latency and performance. Instead of relying on assumptions, you can design architectures that place workloads exactly where they need to be.

This is especially important for applications that demand real-time responsiveness, such as financial platforms, gaming systems, or live streaming services.

### Built-In Compliance Awareness

Compliance and data residency requirements are becoming increasingly complex. Different countries and industries have strict rules about where data can be stored and processed.

The globe experience allows you to filter regions based on compliance capabilities, helping you ensure that your architecture meets regulatory requirements from the start.

This reduces the risk of costly redesigns later and builds trust with stakeholders and customers.

### High Availability and Disaster Recovery

Resilience is a core responsibility for any solution architect. Systems must be designed to handle failures without disrupting users.

Using the globe, you can:

- Identify region pairs
- Plan failover strategies
- Design multi-region deployments

This makes it easier to build architectures that remain available even during outages. Instead of reacting to failures, you’re proactively designing for them.

### Performance Optimization

Performance is not just about compute power it’s also about proximity and network design.

Microsoft’s global infrastructure includes a vast network backbone that connects regions efficiently. By using the globe experience, you can:

- Place services closer to end users
- Reduce latency
- Improve application responsiveness

For global applications, this can make a significant difference in user experience.

### Sustainability as a Design Factor

Sustainability is becoming an important consideration in modern architecture. Organizations are increasingly expected to reduce their environmental impact.

Microsoft has made strong investments in energy efficient datacenters and renewable energy. The globe experience highlights this innovation, allowing architects to factor sustainability into their decisions.

Designing greener systems is no longer optional it’s part of responsible architecture.

## How to Use It in a Real Project

To truly benefit from the Azure Globe experience, it’s important to integrate it into your architecture workflow.

### Step 1: Define Your Requirements

Start by understanding the needs of your project:

- Where are your users located?
- What are your compliance requirements?
- What level of availability do you need?
- What are your performance expectations?

Clear requirements will guide your decisions.

### Step 2: Explore Regions Visually

Use the globe to zoom into relevant geographic areas and identify nearby Azure regions.

This step helps you quickly understand your options and narrow down the best candidates.

### Step 3: Apply Filters

Filter regions based on:

- Compliance and certifications
- Availability zones
- Supported services

This ensures that your chosen regions align with both technical and regulatory needs.

### Step 4: Design for Resilience

Select:

- A primary region
- A secondary (failover) region

Then plan how traffic will be routed between them. This could involve load balancing, replication, and failover mechanisms.

### Step 5: Validate Your Architecture

Finally, evaluate your design against business goals:

- Is it cost-effective?
- Does it meet performance targets?
- Is it scalable?
- Does it align with sustainability goals?

This step ensures your architecture is not only functional but also strategic.

## Real-World Example

Imagine you’re building a fintech platform serving users across Europe, the Middle East, and North Africa.

Using the Azure Globe experience, you can:

- Identify regions close to each user base
- Ensure compliance with regional data regulations
- Design failover between geographically separate regions

The outcome is a system that delivers:

- Low latency for users
- High availability
- Regulatory compliance

This is exactly the kind of architecture modern businesses require.

![](https://dellenny.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/image-6-1024x453.png)
## Key Benefits for Solution Architects

When used effectively, the Azure Globe experience helps you:

✔ Make informed, data-driven decisions ✔ Reduce risks related to compliance and outages ✔ Improve user experience through better performance ✔ Design systems that scale globally ✔ Communicate architecture more clearly to stakeholders

The Azure Globe experience transforms how solution architects approach cloud design. It takes something complex global infrastructure and makes it visual, interactive, and understandable.

Instead of guessing or relying on static documentation, you can explore, compare, and plan in a much more intuitive way.

In a field where small decisions can have massive impacts, tools like this provide a real competitive advantage. If you’re serious about building modern, resilient, and scalable cloud solutions, this is one tool you should not overlook.
